rulings.Case Title: RONALD SVENDSGAARD et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Def. National Steel And Shipbuilding Co. And Foster Wheeler Llcs Motion To Dismiss Action For Delay In Prosecution
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 29, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Coen Company, Inc.¿s joinder in defendants National Steel and Shipbuilding Co. and Foster Wheeler LLC¿s motion to dismiss action for delay in prosecution is denied. There is good cause for the court to refrain from exercising its discretion to dismiss provided by C.C.P. 583.410 and 583.420. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIE HOWARD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 29, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Rosendin Electric, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is taken off calendar as to issues 1, 2 and 3 (negligence, strict liability and false representation) and denied as to issue 4 (premises/contractor liability). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and responses to defendant¿s written contention discovery preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant did not meet its burden of production under the affirmative evidence prong of Aguilar because defendant¿s evidence does not affirmatively negate an essential element of plaintiff¿s claims. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production to make substantial factor causation an issue. Plaintiff has dismissed his causes of action for negligence, strict liability and false representation. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)
